title: Parents feel extra safe with the new school zone spedometers
summary: Syracuse has installed speed-detection signs in school zones to enhance pedestrian safety as the school year begins. The devices display real-time speed warnings to drivers exceeding 25 MPH limits, with CNY Central's field test finding 60% of drivers speeding. After a 60-day grace period starting Wednesday, violations will incur $50 fines.

political_signal.description: Community safety initiative demonstrating proactive traffic enforcement in school zones; positive parental perception suggests constituent support for vision-zero-aligned speed management.

processing_notes: Article focuses on public perception and enforcement mechanism rather than procurement or IT infrastructure details
No budget impact documented beyond stated $50 fine amount
James Street location serves as example; full rollout scope unclear
Grace period end date determinable (60 days from Wednesday following Sept 1, 2025)
